sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 4, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: The agent (green square with white center) moves right by 3 cells per RIGHT press. After 2 RIGHT presses, it's now adjacent to the stationary green square with black center. The agent's green pixels decreased (8→5) and white center expanded (1→4). The two green squares are now touching.
- Goal model: Unclear - may need to merge/collect the green squares or reach the charcoal box on the right panel.
- Action model: RIGHT moves agent right by 3 cells. Need to test other directions.
- Plan: Try RIGHT again to see if agent can pass through the stationary green square, then explore other directions.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 3 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something very interesting! After the 3rd RIGHT press:
Row 30: `gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwNNNwwwwwwppppppwwwNNNccccwwwwwg`
Row 31: `gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwNWNwwwwwwppppppwwwNBNwwwwwwwwwg`
Row 32: `gwwcccccwwwwwwwwNNNwwwwwwppppppwwwNNNwwwwwwwwwg`
The agent (green with white center) is now at cols 24-26 (node 6), and the stationary green square with black center (node 8) has MOVED to cols 42-44! It jumped from cols 27-29 to cols 42-44.
Also, the charcoal square on the right panel (node 4) changed from 16 to 15 pixels, and its boundary changed. The white square (node 9) that was at cols 30-32 is now gone.
So what happened:
1. The agent moved right again
2. When the agent touched the stationary green square with black center, the stationary green square "teleported" or moved to a new position (near the charcoal box on the right panel)
3. The white square between them disappeared
This is a key mechanic! When the agent touches the green square with black center, that green square moves to a new location.
Looking at the new position: the green square with black center is now at cols 42-44, which is right next to the charcoal box at cols 44-48. The charcoal box boundary changed, suggesting the green square is now inside or adjacent to it.
Let me check: node 4 (charcoal) boundary changed from [[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 44]] to [[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 45], [29, 44]]. The bottom-right corner changed, and the pixel count went from 16 to 15. This suggests the green square is overlapping with the charcoal box.
The game mechanic appears to be: the agent pushes or transfers the black-centered green square to the charcoal box target. I should try RIGHT again to see if the agent can reach the charcoal box and complete the level.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Key discovery! When the agent (green with white center) touches the stationary green square (with black center), the stationary green square teleports to a new position near the charcoal box on the right panel. The agent is at cols 24-26, the green square with black center jumped to cols 42-44 (next to the charcoal box). The charcoal box changed shape (16→15 pixels), suggesting overlap.
Goal model: The goal is likely to move the green square with black center INTO the charcoal box on the right panel. The agent acts as a "pusher" that triggers the teleport.
Action model: RIGHT moves agent right by 3 cells. When agent touches the green+black square, it teleports to the next target.
Plan: Try RIGHT again to see if the agent can push the green square into the charcoal box, completing the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try RIGHT again - the agent needs to reach the green square with black center again
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check the ASCII around the agent area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i, line in enumerate(ascii_lines):
    if 29 <= i <= 33:
        print(f"Row {i}: {line[8:55]}")

# Check segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\nSmall objects:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] < 200:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
